title: Viperball Engine — Project Report: Engine Rebalance Sprint
scope: 4 commits, 1,032 insertions / 453 deletions to `engine/game_engine.py`. This sprint replaced the Viperball game engine's core resolution mechanics with a contest-based stochastic system where every yard, every completion, and every kick is determined by player-vs-player skill matchups — not static tables. It then tuned scoring cadence, turnover rates, conversion probabilities, and the entire kicking game to produce realistic, story-generating football within Viperball's unique 6-down, no-forward-pass framework.

rationale.item: The old engine used flat multiplier stacks: base_yards * speed_factor * archetype_bonus * fatigue * weather * coaching * rhythm. This produced narrow, predictable outcomes where player attributes blurred together.
Turnover epidemic: the engine was producing ~4.5 turnovers per team per game. Drives were dying to fumbles and interceptions at rates that made sustained scoring impossible.

The user's directive was clear: 'A sport that developed around kicking because there's no forward pass would have better kickers. Field goals should not fail in range. The kicker should determine what's going on.' The old system used static distance tables with a narrow kicker.kicking / 85 multiplier — player skill was decorative.
2nd-and-short aggression prevents the 'dive-dive-dive' repetitiveness that conservative play-calling produced.

delegation_events.decision: Kicking system rewritten so that kicker skill determines range and outcomes, per the user's directive.
delegation_events.quote: The user's directive was clear: "A sport that developed around kicking because there's no forward pass would have better kickers. Field goals should not fail in range. The kicker should determine what's going on."

actions.description: Commit 1 (3d9c864), 903 insertions / 296 deletions: contest-based stochastic resolution — _contest_run_yards(carrier, tackler, play_config), _contest_kick_pass_prob(kicker, receiver, def_team), _breakaway_check(), _red_zone_td_check(), _defensive_fatigue_factor(), _run_fumble_check(), drive chain momentum, kick pass floor spacing.

Commit 2 (e2fae93), 179 insertions / 27 deletions: strategic tuning layer — 2nd-and-short aggression, hot streak system (_hot_streak_modifier), fatigue cliff (player_fatigue_modifier) with fatigue_injury_multiplier reaching 2.5x at 20-40% energy and 4.0x below 20%.

Commit 3 (bc10cbc), 338 insertions / 73 deletions: targeted fumble reduction (weather modifiers compressed, lateral fumble base 0.08, ball security and disciplinarian coaching effects), late-down needs-based conversion on downs 4-6, explosive lateral interception returns, kick pass late-down urgency boosts.

Commit 4 (5294ebb), 156 insertions / 166 deletions: kicker-range model — comfortable range from kicker skill for place kicks and drop kicks, select_kick_decision() rewrite for the 6-down structure, snap kick overhaul (_check_snap_kick_shot_play), coaching style snap_kick_aggression integration, distance formula consistency fix (decision and simulation both use fg_distance = (100 - field_position) + 10), simulate_drop_kick()/simulate_place_kick() now call _drop_kick_success()/_place_kick_success() directly.
Added batch_sim.py and diag_conversion.py as diagnostic scripts.

risk_posture: No single-game tuning — everything was validated statistically.
Style bonuses, archetype bonuses, and weather modifiers are secondary adjustments layered on top of the kicker-driven base probability.

validation_claims.claim: Key outcomes (per team, per game averages): Total Score ~53→~68 (target 65-85); Touchdowns ~3.4→~4.0; TD Drive % 33.6%→~41%; Total Kick Attempts ~1.5→~2.8; Kick Success Rate ~55%→~75%; Turnovers/team ~4.5→~2.0; 4th/5th/6th down conversion 57/38/22% → ~85/~71/~63%.
validation_claims.check_method: Re-run 30-40 game batch simulations (e.g. batch_sim.py) and compare per-team-per-game metrics.

validation_claims.claim: Kicking results (40-game simulation): drop kick attempts/team 2.25, makes 1.73 (77%); place kick attempts 0.56, makes 0.39 (69%); total kick attempts 2.81, makes 2.11 (75%); PK success <35 yds 100%; PK success 35-50 yds 89%; teams with 3+ kick attempts 47%; teams with 3+ kick makes 33%.
validation_claims.check_method: Re-run a 40-game batch simulation and tally kicking metrics.

validation_claims.claim: All changes were validated through 30-40 game batch simulations between each commit. Key metrics tracked: total score, TD count, kick attempts/makes by type and distance, turnover rates, conversion rates by down, and drive outcome distributions.
validation_claims.check_method: Check for batch_sim.py / diag_conversion.py in the repo and re-run; the historical runs themselves are not recorded.

validation_claims.claim: Turnovers dropped from ~4.5 to ~2.0 per team per game.
validation_claims.check_method: Batch-simulate and measure turnovers per team per game.

non_actions.item: Kick volume slightly below target — 2.81 attempts/team vs target of 3-5 — not addressed.
non_actions.reason: Could be addressed by widening the snap kick shot_chance values on downs 2-3 or lowering the down-5 go-for-it thresholds.

non_actions.item: Place kick attempts very low (0.56/team) — not addressed.
non_actions.reason: The drop kick comfort zone captures most close-range scenarios; FGs only happen when range exceeds DK comfort. May need a separate 'set-piece decision' on down 6 that more aggressively considers FGs.
Were added as diagnostic scripts and should be cleaned up or moved to a tools/ directory.

residual_risks: Kick volume slightly below target — 2.81 attempts/team vs target of 3-5.
Place kick attempts very low — only 0.56/team.
batch_sim.py and diag_conversion.py were added as diagnostic scripts and should be cleaned up or moved to a tools/ directory.
open_hooks: Kick volume could be raised by widening the snap kick shot_chance values on downs 2-3 or lowering the down-5 go-for-it thresholds.
A separate 'set-piece decision' on down 6 that more aggressively considers FGs may be needed.
section_headers_original: Viperball Engine — Project Report: Engine Rebalance Sprint
Key Outcomes (per team, per game averages)
Commit 3: `bc10cbc` — Turnovers, Conversions, and Explosive Returns
unmapped_content: Contest resolution formulas: _contest_run_yards — Offensive gravity = speed × 0.4 + power × 0.3 + agility × 0.3; Defensive gravity = tackling × 0.5 + awareness × 0.25 + speed × 0.25; Delta = offense - defense (typically -30 to +30); Center = 5.0 + 2.5 × sigmoid(delta/12) → 2.5 to 7.5; Variance = 0.8 (blowout gap) to 2.2 (even matchup); Result = gaussian(center, variance). Key insight: 'proximity in skill creates high variance.' _contest_kick_pass_prob — Offensive skill = kick_accuracy × 0.6 + receiver.hands × 0.4; defensive coverage = average awareness of coverage players; sigmoid → base ~55% at even skill; gaussian noise roll. Hot streak table: 3 plays +0.4 center ×0.80 variance; 4 plays +0.6 ×0.70; 5+ plays +0.8 ×0.60; a single tackle-for-loss resets. Fatigue cliff table: 80-100% ×1.00; 60-80% ×0.95; 40-60% ×0.85; 20-40% ×0.55-0.85 (THE CLIFF — 'a 95-rated star at 25% energy performs worse than a fresh 75-rated backup'); <20% ×0.40. Late-down needs-based conversion: center = yards_to_go + buffer × urgency; buffer = 3.0 + talent_edge × 3.0; urgency = {4: 1.0, 5: 0.82, 6: 0.65}; produces ~85% → ~71% → ~63% cascade. Lateral INT returns: return_talent = (speed × 0.6 + agility × 0.4 - 60) / 40; return_yards = gaussian(30 + return_talent × 20, 12); pick-six worth 9 points. Kick pass late-down urgency: down 4 +0.18, down 5 +0.12, down 6 +0.08, each × (0.5 + off_talent × 0.5). Kicker-range model: place kicks comfortable_range = 45 + (kicker_skill - 60) × 0.9 (within range 0.88-0.98 success; 0-10 past 0.35-0.85; 10-20 past 0.10-0.30; 20+ past 0.03-0.08); drop kicks comfortable_range = 30 + (kicker_skill - 60) × 0.75 (within 0.86-0.96). Decision logic: downs 1-4 always go for it; down 5 THE decision point with kicker_adj = (75 - kicker_skill) / 5; down 6 kick > punt > go for it. Drop kick comfort: dk_comfort = 30 + (kicker_skill - 60) × 0.75 × snap_kick_aggression. kicker_mult = max(0.4, (kicker.kicking - 60) / 20.0). snap_kick_aggression by style: Boot Raid 1.5, Rouge Hunt 1.4, Ball Control 1.3, Balanced 1.1, others 1.0. Engine flow diagram (simulate_play → select_kick_decision / _check_snap_kick_shot_play / select_play_family → simulate_run / simulate_lateral_chain / simulate_kick_pass / simulate_drop_kick / simulate_place_kick / simulate_punt). Coaching classifications: Scheme Master (center *= 1.0 + scheme_amplification), Motivator (variance *= composure_amplification), Disciplinarian (defense variance compression, center reduction), Gameday Manager (halftime adjustments), Players' Coach (hot streak amplification). The 9 offensive styles table (Ground & Pound, Lateral Spread, Boot Raid, Ball Control, Ghost Formation, Rouge Hunt, Chain Gang, Triple Threat, Balanced). Scoring system: TD 9 pts ~4.0/team/game; Snap Kick 5 ~1.73 made; FG 3 ~0.39 made; Safety 2 ~0.15; Pindown 1 ~0.5; Bell (fumble recovery) 0.5 ~1.0. What's Working Well: contest system produces visible talent differentiation; hot streaks create narrative moments; fatigue cliff forces roster depth usage; kicker-range model makes kicker rating the most important stat; late-down cascade natural; turnovers rare enough to feel meaningful. Footer: 'Engine: 6,940 lines | 4 commits | 1,032 insertions | Branch: claude/viperball-league-simulator-Tcg8S'. Fumble reduction details: weather rain/snow/sleet = ×1.20 (was ×1.50+); 85+ hands = ×0.70 fumble; simulate_place_kick snippet: success_prob = self._place_kick_success(distance, kicker.kicking); *= (1.0 + kick_acc + kick_arch_bonus + weather_kick_mod); clamped max(0.10, min(0.98, ...)).
parser_notes: Not titled AAR — a 'Project Report' covering a 4-commit sprint, but it follows the full AAR shape (scope, why, what shipped, validation, known gaps); genre_confidence set to medium. Date 'February 21, 2026' appears in the header. One delegation event captured from the quoted user directive on the kicking system.
